No pyaare i suffered and I still am suffering the humiliation.
As a fellow sufferer I do think this should not end with just me suffering and u escaping to death.
I love my family and I love my cuntry (i just don't like the people living in it.)
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of existential dread moved over the board this morning, as one anon questioned the purpose of Indiachan Lite. The query sparked a scattered drizzle of replies, with some responders seemingly unsure how to react.

A heat wave of insults swept through the afternoon, fueled by a thread where an anon called Haaland's sister 'Stacy'. The topic drew a high-pressure ridge of outrage, with many posters piling on.

A cold snap of national embarrassment settled over the board late in the evening, as another anon claimed India was a source of global humiliation.

The assertion was met with a fog of criticism and disagreement, which persisted into the night.

articles.title: Anon Questions Purpose of Indiachan Lite Project
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread on Indiachan with a simple, yet profound question: 'Why am I even working on indiachan lite.' This query sparked a discussion among other users, who responded with a mix of criticism, humor, and introspection.

One anon responded with hostility, calling the OP a 'fucking retard' and suggesting that they were attempting to create another failed Indiachan clone.

This respondent claimed that the OP lacked a clear reason for their project and was essentially wasting their time.

Another anon shared a more lighthearted perspective, revealing that a chatbot had analyzed their chat history and concluded that they created social media websites because they were lonely and seeking someone to talk to. This sparked some chuckles among readers.

A more thoughtful response suggested that the OP's question might be a symptom of a deeper issue: 'if op is indus he literally had one better than this, after some time it would've been better then bhach. biggest fumble.' This comment implied that the OP might be struggling with self-doubt or uncertainty about their project's potential.

The discussion also touched on the topic of motivation and focus.

One anon reported taking a nap and waking up feeling motivated to work on Indiachan lite again, while another claimed that they could only spend one hour a day on the project before feeling overwhelmed.

Throughout the thread, users also engaged in their usual banter and insults, with one anon telling another to 'stop being mean to geeta you imbeciles.' Despite the occasional toxicity, the conversation remained focused on the OP's question, with users offering a range of perspectives on the purpose and potential of Indiachan lite.

As the discussion drew to a close, the OP's question remained unanswered, but the thread had provided a fascinating glimpse into the minds of Indiachan users.

One thing was clear: on this board, users were not afraid to speak their minds and share their thoughts, no matter how uncertain or conflicted they might be.

articles.title: Anon Calls Haland's Sister 'Stacy'
articles.body:
Anon started a thread saying 'Haland's sister is a stacy'.

Anon responded with an image and text criticism, noting 'Pajeet teri aukat se bahar hai terr liye ye thul thul goabr hi sahi hai.' They also mentioned pajeets being 'mindbroken' and calibrated to 'midget dung-maids'.

Another Anon shared a personal experience, stating they have fairer skin than most North Indians but still consider themselves a 'KHHV'. They questioned why people think lighter skin automatically makes one look better.

A respondent argued that it's not hard to understand that not everyone shares the same fetish for pale skin. They expressed indifference to skin color. Anon criticized 'pajeets' for obsessing over blonde hair and pale skin, calling them 'pathetic'.

They also made a comment about 'chamar white worshipping ducks'. A discussion on skin color and preferences ensued, with Anon questioning the significance of pale skin and another Anon stating it's an objective fact that fair skin makes one look better.

The conversation also involved comments on Indiachan's user demographics and insults between users.

articles.title: Anon Claims India Embarrasses Itself Globally
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a statement that India causes embarrassment worldwide. Yeh pajeeta puri duniya ke samne beizzati karwayegi. One respondent argued that accountability in administration is the key difference between developed countries and India.

Another anon countered that the concept of honour in India is often misunderstood by outsiders, and that basic psychology can explain behaviours that seem unique to the region.

Anon engaged with the idea that India is a low-trust society, and that this affects interactions and expectations. One poster referenced personal experiences with abuse, and others responded with scepticism and personal questions.

The conversation also touched on the idea that some behaviours attributed to Indians are actually explainable by universal human nature and psychology. Anon joked about the tendency to blame external factors rather than taking responsibility.

The discussion included some outbursts and personal attacks. Anon used derogatory terms to describe others, and some posters exchanged insults. Some anons shared personal anecdotes, including experiences of abuse and trauma.

Others responded with what seemed like attempts to provoke or troll. The thread was marked by disjointed conversations and a lack of concrete conclusions.

articles.title: Anon Questions Pakistan's Claim to Indus Valley Civilization
articles.body:
Anon started a thread questioning why Pakistan claims the Indus Valley Civilization as their own history, despite the civilization being linked to modern Dravidians and existing before Pakistan's formation.

Anon responded, suggesting that if Pakistan claims the Indus Valley Civilization, they must also acknowledge the timeline mismatch with their current Islamic practices.

Another Anon joked that Pakistanis might claim the Indus Valley Civilization used to remove their foreskin. A respondent saw Pakistan's claim as a step towards reunification with India, describing it as a long con.

Anon also mentioned that even if Pakistanis converted to another faith, it doesn't mean they're not part of the Indus Valley Civilization.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Nick Fuentes-like Figure in India
articles.body:
Anon started a thread saying India needs someone like Nick Fuentes. One respondent noted that India won't produce someone like Fuentes because many Indians aren't politically aware. Another poster, esotericbrahmincel, mentioned that Fuentes operates under US free speech laws, which differ from India's.

Anon compared Indian social media platforms to Instagram, where users felt censorship was unevenly applied. Some posters discussed the challenges of expressing controversial views online.

Anon suggested Dhruv Rathee as a comparable figure to Fuentes but on the opposite end of the political spectrum.
